content.problemStatement:
A set SSS is said to be good iff :


For all x,y,z‚ąąSx, y, z \in Sx,y,z‚ąąS, such that x‚Č†y,x‚Č†z,y‚Č†zx \ne y, x \ne z, y \ne zxÓÄ†=y,xÓÄ†=z,yÓÄ†=z; (x,y,z)(x, y, z)(x,y,z) are the side lengths of a valid non-degenerate triangle.
Count the number of sets SSS of NNN distinct integers which are good, and all elements of the set SiS_iSi‚Äč satisfy 1‚Č§Si‚Č§M1 \le S_i \le M1‚Č§Si‚Äč‚Č§M.

Since the answer may be large, output it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two space-separated integers NNN and MMM ‚ÄĒ the size of the set SSS, and the upper bound on the elements of set SSS.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, a single integer denoting the number of good sets modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of MMM does not exceed 10610^6106 over all test cases.
